channel.title: Devblogs.microsoft.com on .NET Ramblings
channel.description: Recent content in Devblogs.microsoft.com on .NET Ramblings

item.title: Make Visual Studio look the way you want
item.description: Themes are personal. Some of us live in dark mode, some swear by high contrast, and some of us have very strong opinions about that one shade of blue from years ago. The new themes in Visual Studio 2026 are built on Fluent, which gives us a much more consistent and accessible foundation, but we have heard from plenty of you who want more contr Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/visualstudio/make-visual-studio-look-the-way-you-want

item.title: GitHub Copilot modernization for C++ is out of preview
item.description: GitHub Copilot modernization for C++ is out of preview as of Visual Studio 2026 version 18.7. This agent, accessible by typing @Modernize in the Copilot Chat window, can be used to upgrade your projects to use a newer version of the Mic Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/cppblog/ghcp-modernization-available

item.title: Orchestration Patterns for Multi-Agent Systems: Performance and Trade-offs
item.description: Introduction Multi-agent chatbots in large retail organizations face a critical architecture challenge: how to evolve from a modular monolith to a microservices architecture that enables agent reuse across teams and use cases. This post shares lessons from Microsoft's Industry Solutions Engineering (ISE) partnersh Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/ise/coordinator-patterns-multi-agent-systems

item.title: June Patches for Azure DevOps Server
item.description: We are releasing new patches for our self‑hosted product, Azure DevOps Server. We strongly recommend that all customers stay up to date with the latest, most secure version of Azure DevOps Server. The most recent release, Azure DevOps Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/devops/june-patches-for-azure-devops-server-5

item.title: Migrating EWS notifications to Microsoft Graph
item.description: Migrating from the Exchange Web Services (EWS) notification framework, which supports push, pull, and streaming notification types, to the Microsoft Graph subscription model represents a transition toward a unified, stateless, and event-driven framework. This framework supports more than just Exchange data. Although Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/microsoft365dev/migrating-ews-notifications-to-microsoft-graph

item.title: Your agent just scaffolded a project from 2020
item.description: Your agent ran a scaffold command. Project generated, dependencies resolved, no errors. Everything looks fine. Except it's based on the project structure from 2020, and neither you nor the agent noticed. How npx picks the right-but-wrong version When an agent scaffolds a project or runs a CLI tool, it often reaches for Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/blog/your-agent-just-scaffolded-a-project-from-2020

item.title: Windows App Development CLI v0.3.2 — bundling support, smarter initialization, and more
item.description: Windows App Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/ifdef-windows/windows-app-development-cli-v0-3-2-bundling-support-smarter-initialization-and-more

item.title: MSVC Build Tools Preview updates - June 2026
item.description: The MSVC Build Tools Preview is updated regularly with the latest features and fixes from the MSVC development team. This post covers updates from the past month, currently targeting the v14.52 release. This encompasses changes across the compiler frontend, backend, linker, standard library, and related tools. Although you can acquire the M Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/cppblog/msvc-build-tools-preview-updates-june-2026

item.title: Copilot Autofix for GitHub Advanced Security for Azure DevOps
item.description: Over the last few years, we've encouraged customers to move their repositories from Azure Repos to GitHub, where the newest AI-powered and agentic development experiences land first. Migrating isn't equally simple for everyone. A move to GitHub can range from straightforward to a multi-year program, depending on an organization's si Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/devops/copilot-autofix-for-github-advanced-security-for-azure-devops

item.title: Review pull requests without leaving Visual Studio
item.description: Pull request integration in Visual Studio has been one of the most requested Git features. Developers have been asking for a way to open a PR, inspect the changes, discuss feedback, and finish the review without switching to the browser Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/visualstudio/review-pull-requests-without-leaving-visual-studio

item.title: Agents League Hackathon 2026 - Enterprise Agents
item.description: From declarative agents for Microsoft 365 Copilot to fully autonomous multi-agent systems orchestrating complex workflows, developers now have the power to redefine how work gets done. If you're ready to push those boundaries, Agents League Hackathon 2026 is your stage. What is Agents League? Agents Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/microsoft365dev/agents-league-hackathon-2026-enterprise-agents

item.title: Is your agent extension actually working?
item.description: This is the third article in a series about Agent Experience (AX): the practice of making AI coding agents work correctly with your technology. The series covers what you can and can't control in the agent stack, how to measure whether your extensions are helping or hurting, and how to iterate toward better outcomes. You shipped yo Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/blog/is-your-agent-extension-actually-working

item.title: Join us for .NET Day on Agentic Modernization Livestream
item.description: If you’re modernizing .NET apps and want practical ways to move faster without rewriting everything, this event is for you. Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/dotnet/join-us-for-dotnet-day-on-agentic-modernization-livestream

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.124
item.description: Learn what is new in Visual Studio Code 1.124 Read the full article Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/vscode-blog/visual-studio-code-1.124

item.title: Spec-Driven Development: A Spec-First Approach to AI-Native Engineering
item.description: AI has made software delivery faster, but speed alone does not guarantee better outcomes. As teams adopt AI-native development, the real challenge is keeping requirements, design, implementation, and validation aligned so the final result still reflects the original intent. Spec-Driven Development (SDD) addresses this by making structured spe Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/blog/spec-driven-development-ai-native-engineering

item.title: PowerToys 0.100 is here: new Shortcut Guide, Command Palette improvements and much more!
item.description: Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/commandline/powertoys-0-100-is-here-new-shortcut-guide-command-palette-improvements-and-much-more

item.title: .NET 11 Preview 5 is now available!
item.description: Today, we are excited to announce the fifth preview release of .NET 11! This release includes improvements across the .NET Runtime, SDK, libraries, ASP.NET Core, .NET MAUI, C#, Entity Framework Core, and more. Check out the linked release notes below and get started today. [cta-button align="center" text="Download .NET 11 Preview 5" url="ht Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/dotnet/dotnet-11-preview-5

item.title: .NET and .NET Framework June 2026 servicing releases updates
item.description: Welcome to our combined .NET servicing updates for June 2026. Let's get into the latest release of .NET & .NET Framework, here is a quick overview of what's new in our servicing releases: Security improvements .NET and has been refreshed with the latest update as of June 9, 2026. This update contains security and non-security fi Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/dotnet/dotnet-and-dotnet-framework-june-2026-servicing-updates

item.title: Distributed multi-agent systems with Aspire and Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description: Agentic applications stop being simple very quickly. A useful assistant rarely stays as one model call. It needs tools, live data, specialized agents, orchestration, memory, voice or chat entry points, deployment infrastructure, health checks, and observability. That is the story behind the Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/aspire/building-distributed-multi-agent-systems-with-aspire-and-microsoft-agent-framework

item.title: ICYMI: Inside the Microsoft Agent Framework: How we designed a layered SDK
item.description: In case you missed it, the Command Line blog was launched last week and has a great article (by yours truly) about our SDK design philosophy with Microsoft Agent Framework. Check it out: Inside the Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/agent-framework/icymi-inside-the-microsoft-agent-framework-how-we-designed-a-layered-sdk

item.title: Staging Part 2 - Disk Space Optimization
item.description: "Disk space optimizations" is one of the key features of MSIX: Disk space optimizations. With MSIX there is no duplication of files across apps and Windows manages the shared files across apps. The apps are still independent Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/insidemsix/staging-disk-space-optimization

item.title: Copilot Code Reviews for Azure Repos
item.description: Over the last several years, we have encouraged customers to move their repositories from Azure Repos to GitHub to take advantage of the latest AI powered and agentic development experiences that GitHub is delivering. At the same time, we recognize that migrating repositories to GitHub can vary significantly in complexity depending Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/devops/copilot-code-reviews-for-azure-repos

item.title: The Microsoft Company Party where everybody played name tag swap
item.description: I learned from a long-retired Microsoft employee about a Company Party that took place around 1984 or so. The company was small enough that a single party could fit the entire company, but not so small that everybody knew everybody else, so each guest was issued a name tag. During the evening, an unofficial game arose in which peopl Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/oldnewthing/20260609-00/?p=112409

item.title: 🚀React Native Windows v0.83 is here!!
item.description: We’re excited to release React Native Windows 0.83.0, aligned with React Native 0.83.4. React Native Windows v0.83 advances the Fabric Architecture with broader Fabric parity, richer input and automation surfaces, stronger accessibility for hosted XAML islands, and new tooling to measure and protect component performance. Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/react-native/%f0%9f%9a%80react-native-windows-v0-83-is-here

item.title: Microsoft Build 2026 recap: vision, launches, and top sessions
item.description: Microsoft Build 2026 is a wrap, and there was a LOT. Whether you were there in person, streaming live, or catching up now, this recap covers the biggest announcements, top sessions, and everything you need to keep building. 🎯 Vision lead-off: the system around AI is the differentiator Jay Parikh's post, Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/blog/build-recap

item.title: .NET at Microsoft Build 2026: Must watch sessions
item.description: That's a wrap on Microsoft Build 2026! From union types in C# to agentic web apps and AI on the edge with .NET MAUI, this year's event showed how .NET 11 is built for the AI era. Whether you joined live or are catching up on demand, here are the .NET sessions worth your time. [alert type="tip" heading="Tip"]Want to binge the whole lineup? W Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/dotnet/dotnet-at-microsoft-build-2026

item.title: Enterprise Live Migrations: Moving from Azure DevOps Repo to GitHub with minimal disruption
item.description: Over the last several years, we’ve encouraged customers to move their repositories from Azure Repos to GitHub to take advantage of the latest AI-powered and agentic development experiences. For many enterprise teams, however, migrating at scale comes with real constraints. Traditional approaches can require extended downtime - som Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/devops/enterprise-live-migrations
